differences.title: 1. DATA FETCHING
differences.leftDesc: Data fetching primarily in serverless functions or Node.js environment.
differences.rightDesc: Leverages Web Fetch API, accessible on client and server.

differences.title: 2. RENDERING
differences.leftDesc: Offers Static Site Generation, Incremental Static Regeneration, and Server-Side Rendering.
differences.rightDesc: Primarily focuses on Server-Side Rendering and Client-Side Rendering.

differences.title: 3. ROUTING
differences.leftDesc: Page routing is determined by the file structure in the 'pages' directory.
differences.rightDesc: Routing is also file-based, mapped to your file structure.

differences.title: 4. MUTATIONS
differences.leftDesc: Handles data mutations via separate API route handlers.
differences.rightDesc: Uses native HTML forms and progressive enhancement for mutations.

differences.title: 5. STYLING
differences.leftDesc: Supports various styling solutions: CSS Modules, Tailwind CSS, styled-jsx, etc.
differences.rightDesc: Built-in support for CSS Modules with automatic scoping.

differences.title: 6. DATA CACHING
differences.leftDesc: Provides advanced data caching strategies for performance optimization.
differences.rightDesc: Leverages HTTP caching mechanisms for efficient data retrieval.

differences.title: 7. COMPILATION
differences.leftDesc: Traditionally uses Webpack for its build process.
differences.rightDesc: Leverages Vite for faster development builds and optimized production.
